description: Complete HTTP FreeKit as a pixel-perfect clone of HTTP Toolkit — full desktop app with Electron, matching every visual detail, feature, and interaction. Reference repos: httptoolkit/httptoolkit-ui, httptoolkit/httptoolkit-server, httptoolkit/httptoolkit-desktop. Excludes account/subscription management.

userStories.title: Audit and fix all CSS design tokens against HTTP Toolkit styles.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need every CSS variable in styles.css to exactly match HTTP Toolkit's styles.ts so the visual output is pixel-perfect.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetch https://raw.githubusercontent.com/httptoolkit/httptoolkit-ui/main/src/styles.ts and compare every value

All 8 font size tokens match: smallPrintSize=12px, textInputFontSize=13px, textSize=14.5px, subHeadingSize=17px, headingSize=20px, largeHeadingSize=24px, loudHeadingSize=38px, screamingHeadingSize=80px
All named color constants match for dark, light, and high-contrast themes
Box shadow alpha is consistently 0.4 dark / 0.3 light
All border-radius=4px, card padding=20px/15px/30px, sidebar=75px, header/footer=38px, row=32px, resizer=11px
Add any missing CSS variables as named custom properties
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference: httptoolkit-ui/src/styles.ts is the single source of truth for all design tokens

userStories.title: Install Phosphor Icons and replace all sidebar icons
userStories.description: As a user, I want the sidebar to use the exact Phosphor icons HTTP Toolkit uses.
acceptanceCriteria: Install @phosphor-icons/web via npm
Load Phosphor CSS/font in index.html
Replace sidebar SVG icons with Phosphor: Intercept=Plug, View=MagnifyingGlass, Mock=PencilSimple, Send=PaperPlaneTilt, Settings=Gear
Sidebar icon size set to 34px (currently 28px)
Icons use regular weight (not bold or fill)
Verify icons render correctly in browser
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference: httptoolkit-ui/src/icons.ts for icon name mapping

userStories.title: Replace all button and action icons with Phosphor Icons
userStories.description: As a user, I want all UI icons (traffic source, detail actions, mock buttons, etc.) to use Phosphor icons matching HTTP Toolkit.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace traffic source SVGs with Phosphor: browsers=Globe, terminal=Terminal, Docker=Cube, cURL=Terminal, mock=GearSix, import=FolderOpen
Replace detail footer icons: scroll=Eye, pin=Thumbtack (with rotate when unpinned), delete=TrashSimple, resend=PaperPlaneTilt, breakpoint=Pause
Replace mock rule action icons with Phosphor equivalents
Replace send page icons with Phosphor
All icons consistent weight (regular) and size
Verify all icons render correctly in browser
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Install Monaco Editor and create wrapper component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Monaco Editor installed and a reusable wrapper function for rendering code editors.
acceptanceCriteria: Install monaco-editor npm package
Copy Monaco Editor workers to ui/vendor/ or configure to load from node_modules
Serve Monaco assets via Express static middleware
Create a createMonacoEditor(containerId, options) JS function in app.js that initializes a Monaco instance
Options support: language, readOnly, theme, value, minimap, lineNumbers, wordWrap, folding
Create a custom dark theme matching HTTP Toolkit: strings=#4caf7d, keywords=#6e40aa, numbers=#5a80cc, comments=#818490, background=#16181e
Create a custom light theme with matching light values
Editor auto-resizes when container resizes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference: httptoolkit-ui/src/components/editor/base-editor.tsx. Monaco is ~5MB bundled. Workers needed: editor.worker, json.worker, css.worker, html.worker, ts.worker

userStories.title: Replace response body viewer with Monaco Editor
userStories.description: As a user, I want response bodies displayed in a Monaco Editor with syntax highlighting, folding, and search.
acceptanceCriteria: Response body card renders a Monaco Editor instance instead of <pre>
Language auto-detected from content-type: json, html, xml, css, javascript, yaml, plaintext
Body view mode dropdown (JSON/Text/Hex/Image/URL-Encoded) switches Monaco language
Read-only mode enabled for response viewing
Ctrl+F opens Monaco's built-in find/replace
Line numbers shown, minimap hidden by default
Hex view still uses the existing textToHex renderer (not Monaco)
Image content types still render as preview
URL-encoded bodies still render as key-value grid
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Replace request body viewer and Send page editor with Monaco
userStories.description: As a user, I want Monaco Editor for viewing request bodies and editing request bodies in the Send tab.
acceptanceCriteria: Request body card in detail view uses Monaco (read-only)
Send page request body textarea replaced with Monaco Editor (editable)
Send page body format dropdown switches Monaco language
Format/prettify button triggers Monaco's built-in formatter
Ctrl+Enter still sends the request when Monaco is focused
Editor content synced to the send tab state for tab switching
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement virtualized traffic list rendering
userStories.description: As a user, I want smooth scrolling through 100,000+ rows without performance issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace innerHTML-based rendering with a virtual scroll implementation
Only ~60 DOM rows exist at any time (visible + 10 buffer above/below)
Row height fixed at 32px, total scroll height computed from filteredRequests.length * 32
Scrollbar reflects true list size
Clicking a row selects it correctly (map scroll position to request index)
Keyboard navigation (arrows, j/k, PgUp/PgDn, Home/End) works correctly with virtual scroll
Performance: 60fps scroll tested with 10,000+ requests
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Reference: httptoolkit-ui uses react-window FixedSizeList. We need a vanilla JS equivalent. Key concept: container has overflow-y:auto, inner div has height = totalRows * 32, visible rows positioned absolutely based on scrollTop.

userStories.title: Virtual scroll: preserve scroll position and auto-scroll
userStories.description: As a user, I want scroll position preserved across panel switches and auto-scroll to work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Scroll position saved to localStorage when switching away from traffic panel
Scroll position restored when returning to traffic panel
Auto-scroll to bottom when user is near bottom and new requests arrive
Auto-scroll stops when user manually scrolls up
Scroll-to-end button scrolls to bottom and re-enables auto-scroll
selectRequest scrolls the selected row into view (center alignment)
Selection highlight works correctly during scroll
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: WebSocket frame-level interception backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the proxy to parse WebSocket frames and emit them as individual events.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse WebSocket frame headers (FIN, opcode, mask, payload length)
Decode text frames (opcode 0x1) as UTF-8
Pass through binary frames (opcode 0x2) as hex-encoded
Handle ping (0x9) and pong (0xA) frames
Parse close frames (opcode 0x8) for close code and reason
Emit each frame as an event via the onRequest callback with protocol='ws-frame'
Frame events include: direction ('client'|'server'), opcode, payload, timestamp, parentId (the WS connection requestId)
Existing WS connection summary event still emitted on close
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference: WebSocket frame format RFC 6455 Section 5.2. Our proxy-server.js _handleHttpUpgrade currently just pipes bytes. Need to interpose a frame parser.

userStories.title: WebSocket frame display in UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see individual WebSocket messages in the traffic list and detail view.
acceptanceCriteria: WebSocket frames appear as sub-rows under the parent WS connection in traffic list
Sub-rows show: direction arrow (-> or <-), payload preview, opcode name, byte count
Clicking a frame shows detail with full payload (text frames in Monaco, binary in hex)
Stream message list card in detail view shows all frames chronologically
Close frame shows close code and reason text
Frame count shown on the parent WS connection row
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: HTTP/2 upstream with session caching
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the proxy to speak HTTP/2 to upstream servers with connection reuse.
acceptanceCriteria: When forwarding HTTPS requests upstream, attempt http2.connect() first
Cache h2 sessions per origin (host:port) for reuse across requests
Fall back to HTTPS/1.1 if h2 connect fails or server doesn't support h2
h2 pseudo-headers handled correctly (strip from captured headers, add for forwarding)
Session eviction after 60s idle or on error
Protocol field in emitted events shows 'h2' when upstream used h2
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference: httptoolkit-server/src/rules/http-agents.ts. Currently our proxy speaks h1.1 to all upstreams.

userStories.title: TLS failure and tunnel events with detail panes
userStories.description: As a user, I want TLS failures and raw tunnels shown as distinct row types with their own detail views.
acceptanceCriteria: TLS failure rows shown as italic, 28px height, centered text (matching TlsListRow)
TLS failure detail pane shows: hostname, error message, error code, timestamp
Raw tunnel rows shown for CONNECT tunnels where no HTTP follows within 5s
Raw tunnel detail shows: hostname, port, bytes transferred, duration
Each event type uses a different row marker color (TLS error=red, tunnel=grey)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Expandable interceptor card config UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want interceptor cards to expand in-grid to show custom configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Add configComponent property to interceptor definitions
When a card with configComponent is clicked, it expands in the CSS grid spanning multiple columns/rows
Expanded card shows a close button (X) in the top-right
Other cards reflow around expanded card (grid-auto-flow: row dense already set)
Clicking close collapses back to normal size
Docker interceptor uses expandable card to show container list / run instructions
Existing Terminal interceptor uses expandable card to show env var copy instructions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Android ADB interceptor backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a backend interceptor that configures Android devices via ADB for traffic interception.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/interceptors/android-adb-interceptor.js
isActivable() checks if adb command is available (adb version)
getMetadata() returns list of connected devices (adb devices, parse serial + status)
activate(proxyPort, {deviceId}) pushes CA cert and sets proxy on specified device
deactivate() removes proxy settings and CA cert from device
Handles both physical devices and emulators
Register in interceptor-manager.js
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Android ADB interceptor UI with expandable card
userStories.description: As a user, I want an Android interceptor card that shows connected devices and lets me select one.
acceptanceCriteria: Android ADB card shows in intercept grid with phone icon
Clicking expands to show device list (serial, model, status)
Each device has an Activate button
Loading spinner shown while activating
Status pill shows 'Activated' with device name when active
Add to INTERCEPTOR_ICONS, INTERCEPTOR_DESCRIPTIONS, INTERCEPTOR_COLORS
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: JVM/Java interceptor
userStories.description: As a Java developer, I want to attach to running JVM processes to intercept HTTP traffic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/interceptors/jvm-interceptor.js
isActivable() checks if Java is installed (java -version) and jps is available
getMetadata() returns list of running JVM processes (via jps -v)
activate() attaches a Java agent that sets proxy system properties and trusts our CA
Show process list in expandable interceptor card
Register in interceptor-manager.js
Add UI metadata (icon, description, color)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Draft-based mock rule save model
userStories.description: As a user, I want to edit mock rules as drafts and save all changes at once.
acceptanceCriteria: Rule edits stored in memory as drafts, not sent to server until Save clicked
Save All button in mock header activates when there are unsaved changes
Per-rule save button saves individual rule to server
Revert button discards all unsaved changes (reloads from server)
Unsaved rules show visual indicator (orange left border or glow)
New rules created as drafts until explicitly saved
Navigating away from Mock page warns if unsaved changes exist
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Inline rule title editing
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click a rule title to edit it in-place.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking rule title or rename icon turns title into a text input
Input pre-filled with current title (or empty for auto-generated)
Enter confirms the edit
Escape cancels and reverts
Clicking outside the input confirms the edit
Empty title reverts to auto-generated summary text
No more prompt() dialog for rename
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Content perspective toggle for transformed requests
userStories.description: As a user, I want to switch between original and transformed views of a proxied exchange.
acceptanceCriteria: When a request was modified by a mock rule, store both original and transformed data
Show a Transform card at the top of the detail pane for modified requests
Dropdown: 'Show original content', 'Show transformed content', 'Client perspective', 'Server perspective'
Switching updates all detail cards (URL, headers, body)
Visual indicator shows which perspective is active
Non-modified requests don't show the Transform card
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Reference: httptoolkit-ui/src/components/view/http/transform-card.tsx. Need to capture pre-transform data in the proxy.

userStories.title: Electron main process with window management
userStories.description: As a user, I want HTTP FreeKit as a native desktop window.
acceptanceCriteria: Install electron and electron-builder as devDependencies
Create electron/main.js with BrowserWindow creation
Window defaults: 1366x768, min 700x600
Install and use electron-window-state to persist size/position/maximized
Window loads http://127.0.0.1:{apiPort}/ after server starts
Add 'electron' script to package.json that runs electron .
App starts and shows the HTTP FreeKit UI in a native window
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Electron: bundled server auto-start and shutdown
userStories.description: As a user, I want the proxy server to start/stop automatically with the desktop app.
acceptanceCriteria: Electron main process spawns server/src/index.js as child process on app launch
Random auth token generated and passed to server and UI
Server stdout/stderr piped to a log file in app.getPath('logs')
On window close: sends POST /api/shutdown to server
3-second timeout before force-killing the server process
Server port auto-detected and passed to BrowserWindow URL
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Electron: application menu and system tray
userStories.description: As a user, I want a proper menu bar and system tray icon.
acceptanceCriteria: Create electron/menu.js with File, Edit, View, Help menus
File: New Session (Ctrl+Shift+N), Quit (Ctrl+Q / Cmd+Q)
Edit: Undo, Redo, Cut, Copy, Paste, Delete, Select All
View: Zoom In/Out/Reset, Toggle Fullscreen, Toggle Dev Tools, Reload
Help: Documentation (opens browser), About HTTP FreeKit (shows dialog)
Platform-specific: macOS app menu with About/Hide/Quit, Windows has no app menu
System tray icon with context menu: Show/Hide Window, Quit
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Electron: preload script and context bridge
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a secure preload script exposing native APIs to the renderer.
acceptanceCriteria: Create electron/preload.js using contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld
Expose: getDesktopVersion(), getServerAuthToken(), getDeviceInfo()
Expose: selectFilePath(), selectSaveFilePath() for file dialogs
Expose: openContextMenu(items) for native context menus
Expose: restartApp() for app restart
Context isolation enabled, nodeIntegration disabled
All IPC calls validate origin matches expected URL
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Electron Builder: cross-platform packaging config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want build scripts that produce installers for all platforms.
acceptanceCriteria: Create electron-builder.config.cjs with app metadata (name, productName, appId)
Windows config: NSIS installer + portable zip target
macOS config: DMG target with background image placeholder
Linux config: AppImage + deb + rpm targets
Create app icons in all required sizes (16-1024px) or use icon generation tool
Add package.json scripts: build:win, build:mac, build:linux, build (all platforms)
Run build:win successfully and produce a working .exe
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Electron: auto-update system
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to check for and install updates automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Install electron-updater
Check for updates on app launch and every 6 hours
Show toast notification when update is available
Download update in background
Install update on next app restart
Linux: check GitHub releases, show download link (no auto-install)
Update check URL configurable (for self-hosted releases)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference: httptoolkit-desktop uses electron-updater with GitHub releases

userStories.title: Custom theme file upload (.htktheme)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to upload a custom theme file to change the app's colors.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings theme section adds 'Custom' option to the dropdown
Selecting Custom opens a file picker for .htktheme / .htk-theme / .json files
Theme file parsed as JSON with color overrides for CSS variables
Theme color preview: render 10 color swatches showing the theme palette
Invalid files show error toast
Custom theme persisted in localStorage
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Complete ARIA accessibility attributes
userStories.description: As a user with assistive technology, I need full keyboard and screen reader support.
acceptanceCriteria: Traffic table: role=grid, rows have role=row, cells have role=gridcell, aria-rowindex, aria-selected
All icon-only buttons have aria-label describing their function
Collapsible cards have aria-expanded
Mock rule groups have aria-expanded
Tabs (sidebar, send) have role=tablist/tab/tabpanel with aria-selected
aria-activedescendant on traffic list for keyboard navigation
Skip-to-content link as first focusable element
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add remaining missing matcher types
userStories.description: As a power user, I need all ~20 matcher types HTTP Toolkit supports.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Wildcard matcher (matches any request)
Add Hostname matcher (separate from Host — hostname only, no port)
Add MultipartFormData matcher (match by multipart field name/value)
Matcher dropdown shows grouped options: Basic (method, host, path), URL (regex, query, port, protocol), Headers (header, cookie), Body (contains, exact, JSON, regex, form data)
All matchers have appropriate input fields in the editor
All matchers work correctly in the proxy's _evaluateMatcher
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference: httptoolkit-ui/src/model/rules/rule-definitions.ts. We already have 18 matchers, need the remaining ~2-3.

userStories.title: Add Webhook handler and RequestAndResponseBreakpoint
userStories.description: As a power user, I need the remaining handler types.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Webhook handler: sends a copy of the request to a configured URL (fire-and-forget)
Webhook editor fields: target URL, optional custom headers
Add RequestAndResponseBreakpoint: pauses both request and response for editing
Both types implemented in proxy-server.js and app.js
Both appear in the handler dropdown under Advanced optgroup
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add SOCKS4a and SOCKS5h upstream proxy types
userStories.description: As a user behind a SOCKS proxy, I need all SOCKS variants supported.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'socks4a' and 'socks5h' to the upstream proxy type dropdown
Implement SOCKS4a connection (hostname resolved by proxy, not client)
Implement SOCKS5h connection (hostname resolved by proxy, not client)
Both types work for HTTP and HTTPS upstream connections
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Consider using the 'socks' npm package for SOCKS client implementation.

userStories.title: Final visual audit and pixel-perfect polish
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a final pass comparing every screen against HTTP Toolkit screenshots.
acceptanceCriteria: Compare Intercept page layout side-by-side with HTTP Toolkit screenshot
Compare View/Traffic page (empty, with data, with selection) against screenshots
Compare Mock page (empty, with rules, expanded rule) against screenshots
Compare Send page (empty, with request, with response) against screenshots
Compare Settings page against screenshots
Fix any remaining spacing, alignment, color, or font differences
Test responsive layout at 1100px and 768px breakpoints
Test all 4 themes (dark, light, high-contrast, auto)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use HTTP Toolkit's live app at https://app.httptoolkit.tech for comparison screenshots
